industries.name: Pharmaceuticals & Medical Imaging
uses: Iodinated X-ray contrast agents (iohexol/Omnipaque, iodixanol/Visipaque, ioversol/Optiray): water-soluble organic iodine compounds injected intravenously to opacify blood vessels and organs during CT scanning, angiography, and urography — iodine's high atomic number provides X-ray opacity

Povidone-iodine (PVP-I, Betadine): iodine complexed with polyvinylpyrrolidone as a broad-spectrum antiseptic for wound care, surgical scrubs, and pre-operative skin preparation — kills bacteria, viruses, fungi, and spores

Radioactive iodine-131 (¹³¹I): produced in nuclear reactors; used to treat hyperthyroidism and differentiated thyroid cancer — thyroid gland selectively concentrates iodine, delivering localized beta radiation to destroy overactive or malignant thyroid tissue

Potassium iodide (KI) tablets as thyroid-blocking agent in nuclear emergencies — saturating the thyroid with stable iodine prevents uptake of radioactive ¹³¹I released in nuclear accidents (Chernobyl, Fukushima)

Amiodarone (Cordarone): widely used antiarrhythmic drug containing 37% iodine by weight — high iodine content causes thyroid side effects but makes it one of the most effective treatments for ventricular arrhythmia

companies.name: GE Healthcare
companies.use: World's largest medical imaging company. Produces Omnipaque (iohexol) and Visipaque (iodixanol) iodinated contrast agents — among the world's largest-volume injectable pharmaceuticals. Major consumer of medical-grade iodine.

companies.name: Guerbet
companies.use: Major contrast agent producer making Xenetix (iobitridol) and other iodinated agents for CT, angiography, and urography. Pure-play contrast media company supplying hospitals and imaging centers globally.

companies.name: Mundipharma / Avrio Health (Betadine)
companies.use: Major producer of Betadine (povidone-iodine) antiseptic solutions, ointments, and surgical scrubs. Betadine is the world's best-known iodine-based antiseptic brand, used in hospitals and households globally.

industries.name: LCD Polarizing Films
industries.share: ~30% of iodine use — the largest single application
uses: Iodine-doped polyvinyl alcohol (PVA) films are the active polarizing element in all LCD displays — iodine molecules align within the stretched PVA film to absorb light in one polarization direction, creating a linear polarizer
Every LCD panel (smartphone, TV, laptop, monitor, automotive display) contains two polarizing films sandwiching the liquid crystal layer — both polarizers use iodine-PVA as the active component
A typical 65-inch 4K LCD TV uses approximately 3–4 grams of iodine in its polarizing films; a smartphone uses ~0.1g. Global LCD production consumes ~30% of world iodine supply annually

OLED displays do not use polarizing films in the same way — as OLED penetration grows, iodine demand from LCD will gradually decline, but LCD remains dominant in TV and monitor markets through the mid-2020s

companies.name: Nitto Denko
companies.use: World's largest optical film manufacturer. Produces iodine-PVA polarizing films for LCD panels supplied to Samsung, LG, BOE, and virtually every LCD manufacturer. Major consumer of iodine for polarizer production at its Japanese and overseas plants.

companies.name: Sumitomo Chemical
companies.use: Major LCD polarizing film producer. Manufactures iodine-doped PVA polarizers for LCD televisions, monitors, and mobile devices. Supplies polarizing film to major Asian panel makers including BOE and CSOT in China.

companies.name: LG Chem
companies.use: Major polarizing film manufacturer supplying iodine-PVA polarizers primarily to LG Display and Samsung Display for use in LCD television, monitor, and laptop display panels.

industries.name: Industrial Chemicals & Agriculture
uses: Acetic acid production: iodine-based Cativa process (iridium-iodide catalyst) and Monsanto process (rhodium-iodide) use methyl iodide as the reactive intermediate in methanol carbonylation — iodine is the essential co-catalyst ligand
Iodine as biocide in teat dipping solutions (iodophor) for dairy cow udder sanitation — pre- and post-milking teat dips containing iodine prevent mastitis infection in dairy herds globally

Ethylenediamine dihydriodide (EDDI) as iodine nutritional supplement in cattle and poultry feed — prevents iodine deficiency and goiter in livestock; also used as expectorant to treat bovine respiratory disease

Silver iodide (AgI) for cloud seeding weather modification — AgI particles nucleate ice crystal formation in supercooled clouds to induce precipitation; used in hail suppression programs and drought mitigation

companies.name: SQM (Sociedad Química y Minera)
companies.use: World's largest iodine producer. Extracts iodine from caliche nitrate ore and from iodine-rich underground brine (bischofite) in the Atacama Desert, Chile — the world's richest iodine deposit. Chile produces ~65% of world iodine supply.

companies.name: Iofina
companies.use: Extracts iodine from produced water (brine) from natural gas wells in Oklahoma using its IOsorb technology — an innovative approach producing iodine from oilfield brine that would otherwise be waste. Also produces specialty iodine derivatives.

companies.name: Toyota Tsusho (ACE/KANTO NATURAL GAS)
companies.use: Japan is the world's second-largest iodine producer — extracted from iodine-rich natural brine associated with natural gas deposits in Chiba Prefecture. Toyota Tsusho and associated companies produce and export Japanese iodine globally.

industries.name: Nutrition & Animal Feed
uses: Iodized salt: potassium iodate (KIO₃) or potassium iodide (KI) added to table salt at ~20–40 mg/kg — the most successful public health nutrition intervention in history, eliminating iodine deficiency disorder (IDD) and endemic goiter from populations in 130+ countries

Iodine deficiency causes cretinism (severe intellectual disability from fetal hypothyroidism) and goiter — WHO estimates 50 million people are affected by iodine deficiency disorders globally, concentrated in South Asia, Central Africa, and remote mountain regions
Potassium iodide in multivitamins and prenatal supplements for populations not fully covered by salt iodization programs — adequate iodine is critical during pregnancy for fetal brain development
Iodine in infant formula: regulatory requirements mandate iodine fortification of all infant formula globally — infant formula is the primary iodine source for formula-fed infants

companies.name: Morton Salt (K+S Group)
companies.use: Largest US iodized table salt producer. Adds potassium iodide to Morton Iodized Table Salt at the FDA-mandated level. Iodized salt has been the primary vehicle for eliminating iodine deficiency in the US since the 1920s.

companies.name: UNICEF / WHO Salt Iodization Programs
companies.use: Universal salt iodization (USI) programs coordinated by UNICEF, WHO, and GAIN have driven iodization of >90% of household salt in 120+ countries — the primary driver of iodine demand in developing markets and the reason for stable baseline iodine consumption globally.
